sourceMovie.title: Die Hard
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Die Hard for the same reason it broke through in 1988: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. John McTiernan keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Die Hard clicked for you, Nobody (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: RED
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Die Hard clicked for you, RED (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Die Hard clicked for you, The Transporter (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Die Hard?
faq.answer: Try Nobody, RED, The Transporter—each shares DNA with Die Hard while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Die Hard?
faq.answer: Die Hard (1988) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Die Hard part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Die Hard Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
